topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The summons landed on my desk like a dropped stone—heavy, unexpected, and jarring. I stared at the printed notice, the accusation stark black ink against the cream paper. My breath hitched, a sudden, shallow intake that did nothing to steady the tremor starting in my hands. It was about the history paper, the one I’d poured weeks into, and the claim was damning. I reread the highlighted section, my eyes skipping over the familiar phrasing, searching for the discrepancy that wasn't there. A slow burn started in my chest, a hot, prickly feeling that made the air in the room feel thin. I pushed the papers away, the sound of the rustling paper loud in the sudden silence.

[story 2]

When Professor Albright called my name, the sound echoed unnervingly in the lecture hall. I rose slowly, my feet feeling oddly disconnected from the floor. The words—'unauthorized text incorporation'—hit me with a physical jolt. My jaw tightened, and I instinctively smoothed down my sweater, a nervous habit I couldn't shake. I looked across the rows of faces, trying to absorb their expressions, but they all seemed muted, distant. A strange lightness, almost buoyant, lifted in my stomach, quickly followed by a sinking sensation. I swallowed hard, the dryness in my throat making the action difficult. I just wanted to speak, to articulate the sheer disbelief washing over me.

[story 3]

I found the email tucked beneath my study notes. The subject line was formal, the body concise, and the content detailed the alleged academic dishonesty. My fingers hovered over the keyboard, the keys feeling slick and alien beneath my touch. I reread the paragraph three times. Each time, the same wave of disorientation washed over me. My gaze drifted to the window, watching the afternoon sun refract through the dust motes dancing in the air—a distracting, almost hypnotic movement. A slight tremor ran through my left arm. I leaned back in my chair, the leather groaning softly, and let out a breath I hadn't realized I was holding, a sound that was more a small, involuntary gasp than an actual exhale.
